questions.q: The Ahom kingdom ruled the valley of which great river?
questions.explanation: The Ahom kingdom ruled the Brahmaputra valley of Assam.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was the Ahom kingdom centred?
questions.explanation: The Ahom kingdom was centred in present-day Assam in northeastern India.

questions.q: From roughly which century did the Ahom kingdom begin to rule?
questions.explanation: The Ahom kingdom was established in the early 13th century CE.

questions.q: For roughly how long did the Ahom kingdom endure in Assam?
questions.explanation: The Ahom kingdom ruled the Brahmaputra valley for some six centuries.

questions.q: Against which great empire did the Ahoms repeatedly defend their kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms successfully resisted repeated Mughal invasions of Assam.

questions.q: In which part of India was the Ahom kingdom located?
questions.explanation: The Ahom kingdom developed a distinctive culture in the northeast of India.

questions.q: The Ahoms are credited with developing a distinctive system of what?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms developed a distinctive administration and culture in the northeast.

questions.q: Which battle is most famously associated with the Ahom defence against the Mughals?
questions.explanation: The naval Battle of Saraighat in 1671 was a famous Ahom victory over the Mughals.

questions.q: Which celebrated Ahom commander led the defence at the Battle of Saraighat?
questions.explanation: The Ahom general Lachit Borphukan led the decisive naval victory at Saraighat in 1671.

questions.q: The Ahoms are noted for resisting invaders chiefly because they protected which region's autonomy?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms safeguarded the independence of Assam and the northeast from outside conquest.

questions.q: Who is traditionally regarded as the founder of the Ahom kingdom?
questions.explanation: Sukaphaa, a prince who crossed into the Brahmaputra valley, is regarded as the founder of the Ahom kingdom.

questions.q: From which region is the founder Sukaphaa traditionally said to have migrated?
options: The Tai region of present-day Myanmar and beyond
questions.explanation: Sukaphaa is traditionally said to have led a Tai (Shan) migration from the region of present-day Myanmar.

questions.q: What title did the rulers of the Ahom kingdom bear?
questions.explanation: Ahom kings carried the title Swargadeo, meaning lord of heaven.

questions.q: What was the labour and military service system at the heart of Ahom administration called?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms organised manpower through the Paik system of obligatory service to the state.

questions.q: Which historical chronicles, written in the Ahom and Assamese languages, record the kingdom's history?
questions.explanation: The Buranjis are the distinctive Ahom historical chronicles documenting the kingdom.

questions.q: Which city served as a principal capital of the Ahom kingdom in its later centuries?
questions.explanation: Rangpur, in the Sivasagar area, was a major Ahom capital in the later period.

questions.q: The Ahom ruler Suhungmung is also known by which adopted Hindu title?
questions.explanation: Suhungmung, an expansionist Ahom king, is also remembered as the Dihingia Raja.

questions.q: The tank-and-tomb monuments called Maidams, built by Ahom royalty, are found near which town?
questions.explanation: The royal Ahom burial mounds, or Maidams, are concentrated at Charaideo in Assam.

questions.q: Over the centuries, the Ahom court gradually adopted which religion and Assamese culture?
questions.explanation: The Ahom rulers progressively adopted Hinduism and the Assamese language and culture.

questions.q: Which Ahom king built the famous Rang Ghar amphitheatre and Talatal Ghar?
options: Rudra Singha and his successors of the 18th century
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar and Talatal Ghar were built under 18th-century Ahom kings such as Rudra Singha and Pramatta Singha.

questions.q: The Battle of Saraighat was fought on which river near present-day Guwahati?
questions.explanation: Saraighat was a naval battle fought on the Brahmaputra near Guwahati in 1671.

questions.q: Which Mughal-aligned Rajput general led the imperial forces against the Ahoms at Saraighat?
questions.explanation: Raja Ram Singh I of Amber commanded the Mughal army defeated by the Ahoms at Saraighat.

questions.q: Which earlier 17th-century battle saw the Mughals briefly capture the Ahom capital before being driven out?
questions.explanation: After Mughal occupation of the region, the Ahoms decisively recovered ground at the Battle of Itakhuli in 1682.

questions.q: The Moamoria rebellion that gravely weakened the Ahom kingdom occurred in which century?
questions.explanation: The Moamoria rebellion of the late 18th century badly destabilised the Ahom state.

questions.q: The Ahom kingdom finally collapsed largely as a result of invasions by which neighbouring power in the early 19th century?
questions.explanation: Repeated Burmese invasions in the early 19th century broke the weakened Ahom kingdom.

questions.q: Which 1826 treaty ended Burmese occupation of Assam and brought the region under British control?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Yandabo in 1826 ended the First Anglo-Burmese War and ceded Assam to the British.

questions.q: What was the original Tai-derived language of the Ahoms, used in many early Buranjis?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms originally used the Tai-Ahom language, later largely replaced by Assamese.

questions.q: Which three high ministers formed the great council of state in the Ahom system?
questions.explanation: The Ahom council of leading ministers comprised the Borgohain, Burhagohain and (later) Borpatrogohain.

questions.q: The royal Ahom burial mounds at Charaideo were in 2024 inscribed as what?
questions.explanation: The Charaideo Maidams were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2024.

questions.q: Lachit Borphukan held which office in the Ahom administration when he led the defence of Saraighat?
questions.explanation: Lachit served as Borphukan, the Ahom commander-governor of the western frontier, during the Saraighat campaign.
